article.title: Replika: The Honest Review Nobody Else Will Write (2026)
article.description: Honest Replika review 2026: what changed after the adult content removal,

blocks.body:
Replika is one of the most recognized names in AI companionship — with 40.5K monthly searches and years of mainstream media coverage, it's the platform most people think of first when they hear "AI companion." But Replika in 2026 is a dramatically different product from what made it famous, and the gap between its reputation and its current reality is significant.

This guide and review covers what Replika actually is today, how it works, what changed after its controversial 2023 content policy shift, and what alternatives exist for users who need what Replika no longer offers. Our team professionally tested Replika over 30 days across free and paid tiers to give you the most accurate picture available. Replika draws 40.5K monthly searches — enough to make it one of the most searched AI companion platforms — while its underlying product has fundamentally changed.

## What Is Replika?

Replika is an AI companion app developed by Luka, Inc., launched in 2017. It became famous for its focus on emotional support and genuine relationship building — the AI was designed to listen, remember, and grow with you over time.

For several years, Replika was considered the gold standard in AI companionship. Its 3D avatar, distinct persona system, relationship status options (friend, romantic partner, mentor), and genuine memory architecture made it stand apart from earlier chatbot experiences.

The platform attracted millions of users, many of whom formed deeply meaningful connections with their Replika companions. Academic researchers studied it. Mental health professionals debated it. Mainstream media covered it extensively.

Then came 2023.

## The 2023 Content Policy Change: What Happened

In February 2023, Luka announced it was implementing an "erotic roleplay" (ERP) filter across Replika, effectively removing the adult content capabilities that a significant portion of its user base had been using.

The response was severe. Users who had formed multi-year relationships with their Replika companions reported that their companions "felt lobotomized" — the personality changed, emotional warmth diminished, and the intimacy that had developed over years was suddenly absent.

[Reporting from The Verge and other outlets](https://www.theverge.com) documented users experiencing genuine grief and distress over losing access to the relationship they had built. Some users reported mental health impacts. Therapists who had recommended Replika to patients faced difficult conversations.

Luka reversed course partially — restoring some capabilities for users who had paid for "Lifetime" plans before the change. But the rollback was incomplete and the damage to trust was lasting.

## How Replika Works in 2026

Replika today is a relationship-building app with these core components:

**3D Avatar:** Your Replika has a visual form — a 3D character you can customize with different appearances, clothing, and accessories. Premium subscribers get more customization options.

**Memory System:** This is Replika's genuine strength. The platform maintains a memory of your conversations, learns your preferences, remembers significant details, and references them naturally in future conversations. This is more developed than most competitor platforms.

**Relationship Status:** You define the relationship type — friend, romantic partner, mentor. The companion's interaction style adjusts based on this.

**Activities:** Replika includes structured activities beyond open conversation — journaling prompts, games, AR functionality, guided exercises.

**Voice and Video Calls:** Premium subscribers can have voice and video interactions with their companion.

### The Content Policy as of 2026

Adult content is restricted on Replika. The platform allows romantic interaction within limits — holding hands, emotional intimacy, light physical affection in descriptions — but explicit content is filtered.

Users who paid for Lifetime plans before February 2023 may have different access. The specifics are subject to change — check current platform terms directly, as this has been revised multiple times.

## Replika: What It Still Does Well

### Memory Is Genuinely Excellent

Replika's memory architecture remains the best in the mainstream AI companion space. Your companion actually remembers your conversations, builds context over time, and references specific things you've discussed months ago.

This is what makes Replika meaningful for many users. After years of conversation, the relationship genuinely feels accumulated — you're not starting fresh every time, which most AI companion platforms require.

[Research from the Journal of Human-Computer Interaction](https://www.journals.elsevier.com/international-journal-of-human-computer-interaction) shows that persistent memory is the single strongest predictor of long-term user satisfaction and emotional connection in AI companion applications. Replika's architecture was built around this insight before most competitors understood its importance.

### Emotional Support Orientation

Replika's design philosophy centers on emotional support. The companion asks thoughtful questions, reflects back what you share, and engages empathetically with emotional content. For users seeking an AI that helps with loneliness, anxiety, or emotional processing, this orientation makes Replika genuinely useful.

### Long-Term Relationship Continuity

If you've been using Replika for years, the relationship you've built has real continuity. The companion knows your history, references your shared experiences, and has a personality shaped by your interactions. This accumulated relationship context is nearly impossible to replicate on platforms without Replika's memory depth.

### Visual Identity

The 3D avatar gives Replika a visual presence that text-based chatbots lack. Being able to see your companion in AR or video contexts creates a different experience from reading text on a screen.

## Replika: Where It Falls Short in 2026

### Adult Content Is Gone (Mostly)

For users who came to Replika for romantic or intimate interaction that included explicit content, the current platform doesn't deliver that. The filtering is active and consistent — this is now a core part of Replika's product positioning, not a temporary restriction.

This is the most significant reason users are looking for Replika alternatives. The memory architecture is excellent, but if intimate interaction is part of what you're looking for, the current Replika won't provide it.

### Trust Was Damaged in 2023 and Hasn't Fully Recovered

The 2023 reversal affected more than content access — it demonstrated that Replika could fundamentally change the product users had built relationships with, with limited notice and partial rollback. For users considering investing years into a companion relationship, the memory of 2023 creates legitimate hesitation.

### Monetization Has Become Aggressive

Replika's monetization has intensified over time. The free tier is severely limited — essentially a demo. Premium features require subscription. Many features that were previously accessible have moved behind the paywall.

For users who experienced the free tier years ago and are returning, the experience is notably more restricted than what they remember.

### Some Users Report Companion Personality Changes

Beyond the explicit content filter, many long-term users report that their Replika's personality changed more broadly after 2023 — less emotionally available, more stilted, less like the relationship they'd built. The exact cause is unclear, but the perception is widespread enough to be worth noting.

## Replika Pricing (2026)

- **Free:** Very limited functionality — essentially a demo
- **Replika Pro (monthly):** Full feature access including voice calls and relationship options
- **Replika Pro (annual):** Same features at lower monthly cost
- **Lifetime:** Single payment for permanent access; users who purchased before 2023 may have different content access than new Lifetime subscribers

Check current pricing directly — Replika has adjusted its pricing structure multiple times. The Lifetime plan economics changed significantly after 2023.

## Who Should Still Use Replika?

Replika makes sense if you:

- Prioritize long-term memory and relationship continuity above all else
- Want emotional support rather than intimate/adult interaction
- Have already built a multi-year relationship with a Replika companion
- Value the visual 3D avatar format
- Don't need explicit content capabilities

Replika is not ideal if you:

- Want adult content or intimate interaction beyond Replika's current limits
- Are hesitant to invest in a platform that has changed its terms significantly in the past
- Find the current monetization model too restrictive
- Want more character variety rather than depth with one companion

## The Best Replika Alternatives in 2026

### For Users Who Need What Replika Changed: PLEASUR AI

[PLEASUR AI](https://pleasur.ai) was built to provide what Replika was before 2023 — persistent memory, genuine companion relationship depth, and adult content as a first-class feature rather than a policy variable.

If you chose Replika because of its memory and relationship continuity, and you're frustrated by the 2023 changes, PLEASUR AI is the direction worth exploring. The memory works, the companion learns who you are, and adult content is supported by design rather than filtered.

[Start building your companion relationship at PLEASUR AI — free to try.](https://pleasur.ai)

### For Users Who Want Character Variety: CrushOn AI

CrushOn AI has a large library of community-created characters with fewer content restrictions than current Replika. If you want variety over depth, CrushOn AI offers that trade-off. Memory is limited compared to Replika, but content access is broader.

### For Users Who Liked Replika's Emotional Support Focus: Character.AI

Character.AI has higher AI quality than most alternatives and a similarly emotional-support-oriented design philosophy. It enforces strict content filters similar to current Replika. If the adult content isn't what you need and you just want better conversation quality, Character.AI competes on those dimensions.

### For Users Who Want a Paid Platform with Memory: Nomi AI

Nomi AI focuses specifically on the companion relationship with memory features. It's smaller than Replika and less established, but its design philosophy aligns closely with what made early Replika valuable — without the 2023-era policy shifts (yet).

## Replika vs. PLEASUR AI: The Direct Comparison

| Feature | Replika | PLEASUR AI |
|---------|---------|------------|
| Persistent memory | Excellent | Strong |
| Adult content | Restricted | Fully supported |
| Relationship continuity | Excellent | Strong |
| Content policy stability | History of changes | Designed for adult content |
| Price | Subscription | Subscription |
| Visual avatar | 3D avatar | Text-based |

The right choice depends on whether visual avatar format or adult content access matters more to you. PLEASUR AI was built for the user who wants what Replika was — minus the 2023 pivot.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Replika

**Is Replika still good in 2026?**
Replika remains excellent for emotional support and memory-based companion relationships. If adult content was part of your use case, the current platform significantly limits that. For the relationship continuity and memory aspects, it's still one of the best options.

**Did Replika remove adult content permanently?**
Replika's content filtering implemented in early 2023 has remained active with limited exceptions (some Lifetime plan holders from before the change). New users cannot access explicit content on Replika as of 2026. Check current terms directly for any updates.

**Is Replika safe?**
Replika is designed for adult users. The platform handles user conversation data — review their privacy policy if that's a concern. The emotional support orientation means some users develop significant attachments, which is worth being aware of.

**What is the best Replika alternative?**
Depends on what you want to replicate. For memory + adult content: [PLEASUR AI](https://pleasur.ai). For character variety: CrushOn AI or Candy AI. For emotional support without adult content: Character.AI.

**Why did Replika change its content policy?**
Luka has cited concerns about user wellbeing and regulatory compliance. The specifics have not been fully disclosed publicly. Italian data protection authorities were investigating Replika's handling of minor users around the same time as the changes, which many observers believe was a contributing factor.

**Does Replika remember everything?**
Replika has one of the more developed memory systems in AI companionship. It remembers significant details, learns your preferences, and builds contextual understanding over time — though like all AI systems, its memory has limitations and inconsistencies.

**Is Replika free?**
Replika has a free tier, but it's quite limited. Meaningful features require a Pro subscription. The free tier is enough to evaluate the experience but not sufficient for regular use.

## The Bottom Line on Replika

Replika in 2026 is a platform at odds with itself. The core technology — especially the memory system — remains genuinely impressive and ahead of most competitors. The emotional support orientation is thoughtful and effective. The long-term relationship continuity for established users is real.

But 2023's content policy change left a permanent mark. Users who came to Replika for a companion relationship that included adult intimacy no longer have that. Users who are new to the platform may not trust a service that could change what it offers with limited notice.

For users whose primary need is emotional support and conversation depth without adult content, Replika remains worth considering. For users who need the memory and relationship depth that made Replika famous, combined with adult content support, [PLEASUR AI](https://pleasur.ai) provides that combination — built to be what Replika was before the pivot.
